content.claim: Ethnic and social segregation in Hungary shows a school-level correlation of 0.79 between disadvantage share and Roma share, with approximately one-third of Roma students in highly segregated environments (schools with >50% Roma enrollment), and Hungary ranks in the bottom 10% of OECD countries in the correlation between sociocultural background and school performance.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Finnish system reform lens
limitations: Segregation patterns result from multiple causal pathways (residential, social, institutional) that cannot be fully separated
Causality between school segregation and selection mechanisms requires additional evidence beyond correlation
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
